name: ISO/IEC 27019:2017
fullName: Information Security Controls for the Energy Utility Industry
description: Energy-sector specific ISO guidelines directing secure controls for electric, gas, and water networks.

questions.name: ISO/IEC 27019:2017 - Utility Information Security
questions.text: Are energy utility information security guidelines formally documented and active?
questions.description:
Review energy security strategies, policy booklets, and administrative approval files.

SOP:
1. Enforce strict role-based access controls (RBAC) separating administrative tasks from standard operator routines.
2. Route all incoming remote connections through isolated administrative Jump Hosts with visual session logging active.
3. Conduct quarterly access audits to identify and completely disable dormant or inactive accounts.
4. Map controls to the internal Statement of Applicability (SoA) register.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the utility security policy configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: ISO compliance evidence must include: Statement of Applicability (SoA), Annex A control validation logs, and Internal Audit reports aligned with ISO ISO 27019 requirements.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
General IT-OT convergence increases the threat landscape by bridging air-gapped industrial facilities with internet-facing corporate systems. Failing to enforce strict regulatory controls risks introducing severe operational vulnerabilities.

questions.name: ISO/IEC 27019:2017 - Process Control Isolation
questions.text: Are process control networks logically isolated from utility business links (enforced by Cisco Industrial Ethernet switches, network zoning firewalls, and isolated Purdue model level boundaries)?
questions.description:
Inspect edge firewall setups, router maps, and isolated DMZ conduits.

SOP:
1. Deploy endpoint protection agents configured with real-time process monitoring to block unsigned scripts and execution threats.
2. Enforce automatic session logout GPOs terminating interactive operator connections after a defined period of inactivity.
3. Configure system event log forwarding to stream all reboots, login attempts, and administrative modifications to a centralized syslog receiver.
4. Map controls to the internal Statement of Applicability (SoA) register.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the network segmentation configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: ISO compliance evidence must include: Statement of Applicability (SoA), Annex A control validation logs, and Internal Audit reports aligned with ISO ISO 27019 requirements.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
Inadequate network segmentation allows IT-OT convergence traffic to flow unmediated across enclaves. A malware infection on the corporate LAN (like ransomware) can propagate directly to critical process control loops, halting operations.

questions.name: ISO/IEC 27019:2017 - HMI Logic Port Hardening
questions.text: Are HMI terminals configured to block unauthorized outbound logical connections (covering Siemens S7-1500 PLCs, Allen-Bradley ControlLogix, SEL RTUs, and digital relay modules)?
questions.description:
Verify local host-based firewall configurations, routing rules, and outbound rules.

SOP:
1. Establish physical locking covers and secure enclosures around critical field device interfaces.
2. Configure hardware configuration locks and disable local diagnostic ports (USB, RS-232) to block local unauthorized adjustments.
3. Validate that device configuration changes require double-signature supervisor tokens before logical modifications are written to memory.
4. Map controls to the internal Statement of Applicability (SoA) register.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the host device hardening configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: ISO compliance evidence must include: Statement of Applicability (SoA), Annex A control validation logs, and Internal Audit reports aligned with ISO ISO 27019 requirements.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
Using unhardened or unpatched field controllers opens critical hardware interfaces to remote execution exploits. Attackers can leverage known vulnerabilities to flash unauthorized firmware or change safety threshold parameters on active PLCs.

questions.name: ISO/IEC 27019:2017 - Telemetry Link Encryption
questions.text: Is telemetry data encrypted traversing external physical lines (utilizing VPN tunnels, encrypted Modbus/DNP3 secure protocols, and HSM keys)?
questions.description:
Verify telemetry VPN configurations, inspect crypto parameters, and check connection logs.

SOP:
1. Deploy endpoint protection agents configured with real-time process monitoring to block unsigned scripts and execution threats.
2. Enforce automatic session logout GPOs terminating interactive operator connections after a defined period of inactivity.
3. Configure system event log forwarding to stream all reboots, login attempts, and administrative modifications to a centralized syslog receiver.
4. Map controls to the internal Statement of Applicability (SoA) register.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the data link protection configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: ISO compliance evidence must include: Statement of Applicability (SoA), Annex A control validation logs, and Internal Audit reports aligned with ISO ISO 27019 requirements.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
Traversing industrial telemetry in cleartext across converged networks invites eavesdropping and packet injection. Malicious actors can execute Man-in-the-Middle (MitM) attacks, spoofing HMI screens while sending dangerous control commands.

questions.name: ISO/IEC 27019:2017 - Substation Entry Controls
questions.text: Are physical access perimeters and entry locks active at substations (covering Siemens S7-1500 PLCs, Allen-Bradley ControlLogix, SEL RTUs, and digital relay modules)?
questions.description:
Verify physical lock gates, check badge-reader entry logs, and review camera feeds.

SOP:
1. Establish physical locking covers and secure enclosures around critical field device interfaces.
2. Configure hardware configuration locks and disable local diagnostic ports (USB, RS-232) to block local unauthorized adjustments.
3. Validate that device configuration changes require double-signature supervisor tokens before logical modifications are written to memory.
4. Map controls to the internal Statement of Applicability (SoA) register.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the physical asset safety configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: ISO compliance evidence must include: Statement of Applicability (SoA), Annex A control validation logs, and Internal Audit reports aligned with ISO ISO 27019 requirements.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
Using unhardened or unpatched field controllers opens critical hardware interfaces to remote execution exploits. Attackers can leverage known vulnerabilities to flash unauthorized firmware or change safety threshold parameters on active PLCs.

questions.name: ISO/IEC 27019:2017 - Operator Session Timeouts
questions.text: Do operator SCADA workstations enforce automatic session locks after 10 minutes?
questions.description:
Verify automated session lock parameters in the SCADA control software interface.

SOP:
1. Deploy endpoint protection agents configured with real-time process monitoring to block unsigned scripts and execution threats.
2. Enforce automatic session logout GPOs terminating interactive operator connections after a defined period of inactivity.
3. Configure system event log forwarding to stream all reboots, login attempts, and administrative modifications to a centralized syslog receiver.
4. Map controls to the internal Statement of Applicability (SoA) register.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the session security controls configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: ISO compliance evidence must include: Statement of Applicability (SoA), Annex A control validation logs, and Internal Audit reports aligned with ISO ISO 27019 requirements.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
General IT-OT convergence increases the threat landscape by bridging air-gapped industrial facilities with internet-facing corporate systems. Failing to enforce strict regulatory controls risks introducing severe operational vulnerabilities.

questions.name: ISO/IEC 27019:2017 - Supply Chain Vetting Logs
questions.text: Are utility hardware components verified for physical authenticity prior to deployment?
questions.description:
Verify physical inspection registers, verify chassis seal numbers, and check chip codes.

SOP:
1. Enforce strict role-based access controls (RBAC) separating administrative tasks from standard operator routines.
2. Route all incoming remote connections through isolated administrative Jump Hosts with visual session logging active.
3. Conduct quarterly access audits to identify and completely disable dormant or inactive accounts.
4. Map controls to the internal Statement of Applicability (SoA) register.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the supply chain risk management configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: ISO compliance evidence must include: Statement of Applicability (SoA), Annex A control validation logs, and Internal Audit reports aligned with ISO ISO 27019 requirements.

General IT-OT convergence increases the threat landscape by bridging air-gapped industrial facilities with internet-facing corporate systems. Failing to enforce strict regulatory controls risks introducing severe operational vulnerabilities.

questions.name: ISO/IEC 27019:2017 - Disaster Recovery Failover Plan
questions.text: Is an active energy infrastructure disaster recovery failover plan tested annually (aligned with incident response playbooks, offsite backups, and isolated write-once media)?
questions.description:
Review failover test logs, check backup server registers, and inspect procedures.

SOP:
1. Deploy endpoint protection agents configured with real-time process monitoring to block unsigned scripts and execution threats.
2. Enforce automatic session logout GPOs terminating interactive operator connections after a defined period of inactivity.
3. Configure system event log forwarding to stream all reboots, login attempts, and administrative modifications to a centralized syslog receiver.
4. Map controls to the internal Statement of Applicability (SoA) register.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the operations recovery configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: ISO compliance evidence must include: Statement of Applicability (SoA), Annex A control validation logs, and Internal Audit reports aligned with ISO ISO 27019 requirements.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
Failing to maintain isolated, offline backups during convergence events risks catastrophic downtime during ransomware outbreaks. If backups reside on the shared enterprise domain, the same malware that encrypts SCADA HMIs will wipe the recovery configurations.

questions.name: ISO/IEC 27019:2017 - Incident Reporting Rules
questions.text: Are operational cybersecurity incidents reported immediately to grid authorities (aligned with incident response playbooks, offsite backups, and isolated write-once media)?
questions.description:
Review regulatory reporting rules, check ES-ISAC notification forms, and verify contact guides.

SOP:
1. Enforce strict role-based access controls (RBAC) separating administrative tasks from standard operator routines.
2. Route all incoming remote connections through isolated administrative Jump Hosts with visual session logging active.
3. Conduct quarterly access audits to identify and completely disable dormant or inactive accounts.
4. Map controls to the internal Statement of Applicability (SoA) register.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the incident communication configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: ISO compliance evidence must include: Statement of Applicability (SoA), Annex A control validation logs, and Internal Audit reports aligned with ISO ISO 27019 requirements.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
Failing to maintain isolated, offline backups during convergence events risks catastrophic downtime during ransomware outbreaks. If backups reside on the shared enterprise domain, the same malware that encrypts SCADA HMIs will wipe the recovery configurations.

Inspect the vendor operations control configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: ISO compliance evidence must include: Statement of Applicability (SoA), Annex A control validation logs, and Internal Audit reports aligned with ISO ISO 27019 requirements.
